mission.title: Install and Configure kubeadm (Kubernetes bootstrapper)
mission.description: Kubeadm is a tool designed to simplify the process of creating and managing Kubernetes clusters. It provides a straightforward way to bootstrap a secure and minimal viable cluster, making it ideal for users who want to set up Kubernetes without extensive configuration. Key trade-offs include the need for manual node management and the requirement for a compatible operating system.

steps.title: Install Kubeadm
steps.description:
This step installs the kubeadm binary on your system, which is necessary for bootstrapping the Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

curl -s https://packages.cloud.google.com/apt/doc/apt-key.gpg | sudo apt-key add -

echo 'deb https://apt.kubernetes.io/ kubernetes-xenial main' | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/kubernetes.list

sudo apt-get update

sudo apt-get install -y kubeadm=1.31.0-00 kubelet=1.31.0-00 kubectl=1.31.0-00

```

> Add the Google Cloud public key.
> Add the Kubernetes APT repository.
> Update the package list.
> Install specific versions of kubeadm, kubelet, and kubectl.
commands: echo 'deb https://apt.kubernetes.io/ kubernetes-xenial main' | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/kubernetes.list
sudo apt-get install -y kubeadm=1.31.0-00 kubelet=1.31.0-00 kubectl=1.31.0-00

steps.title: Initialize the Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command initializes the control-plane node of your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

sudo kubeadm init --kubernetes-version=v1.31.0 --pod-network-cidr=192.168.0.0/16

```

> Initialize the cluster with a specific Kubernetes version and pod network CIDR.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures kubectl to use the new cluster by setting up the kubeconfig file.

```bash

mkdir -p $HOME/.kube

sudo cp -i /etc/kubernetes/admin.conf $HOME/.kube/config

sudo chown $(id -u):$(id -g) $HOME/.kube/config

```

> Create the kube directory.
> Copy the admin config to the user's kube directory.
> Change ownership of the kube config file.

steps.title: Install a Pod Network Add-on
steps.description:
This step installs a network add-on to enable communication between pods.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://docs.projectcalico.org/manifests/calico.yaml

```

> Install Calico as the network plugin.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in the cluster to ensure they are ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes and their statuses.

uninstall.title: Reset Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command will remove all Kubernetes components and reset the cluster state. Data loss may occur.

```bash

sudo kubeadm reset

sudo rm -rf $HOME/.kube

```

> Reset the kubeadm cluster.
> Remove kube config to clean up user settings.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
Backup the current cluster state, upgrade the cluster, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get nodes -o wide > nodes-backup.txt

sudo kubeadm upgrade plan

sudo kubeadm upgrade apply v1.31.0

kubectl get nodes

```

> Backup current node states.
> Check available upgrades.
> Upgrade to the new Kubernetes version.
> Verify that nodes are updated.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubelet not running'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubelet service may not be started or is failing.

**Fix:**
Run 'sudo systemctl start kubelet' to start the kubelet service.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubeadm init failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to insufficient resources or incorrect configurations.

**Fix:**
Ensure your system meets the minimum requirements and check the kubeadm config.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate is valid for ...'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates a certificate issue, often due to an expired or misconfigured certificate.

**Fix:**
Regenerate certificates using 'kubeadm certs renew all'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'node not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the network plugin is not installed or configured properly.

**Fix:**
Ensure that a network add-on is installed and configured correctly.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new features such as improved scheduling and enhanced logging.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.30, ensure to check the new scheduling policies.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of nodes with 'Ready' status when running 'kubectl get nodes'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure your system meets the resource requirements and has the necessary tools installed.
